title: restore_failure
description: A backup restore fails during a critical recovery operation, requiring alternative recovery methods.

timeline.title: Restore Initiated
timeline.description: Following a server failure, the team initiates restoration from the most recent backup.

timeline.title: Restore Job Fails
timeline.description: The restore job fails with a checksum mismatch error. The backup archive is corrupt.

timeline.title: Previous Backup Attempted
timeline.description: The team attempts to restore from the previous backup, which also fails — it was created from the same corrupted source.

timeline.title: Oldest Clean Backup Found
timeline.description: A third backup from 5 days ago passes integrity checks and is available for restore.

timeline.title: 5-Day-Old Backup Restoration
timeline.description: Restoration from the 5-day-old backup proceeds. The 5-day data gap must be addressed.

timeline.title: Data Gap Reconciliation
timeline.description: Transaction logs are used to replay 3 of the 5 days of missing data. Remaining 2 days recovered from application logs.

node-1.title: Backup Restore Failure
node-1.situation: During an emergency recovery, the most recent backup restore fails with a checksum mismatch. The previous backup also fails. You have discovered the backup system has not been performing integrity verification on created backups. You need to find a clean backup and assess recovery options.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Systematically test older backup archives for integrity starting with the most recent, identify the oldest clean backup available, and in parallel explore what data can be recovered from other sources (transaction logs, replicas).

options.label: B
options.text: Declare the data unrecoverable and begin rebuilding the system from scratch without exhausting all backup options.

options.label: C
options.text: Attempt to repair the corrupted backup archive before testing older backups.

node-2.title: Clean Backup Identified
node-2.situation: A backup from 5 days ago passes integrity checks and is ready for restoration. Transaction logs from the database are available for days 1-3 of the gap. For days 4-5, you have application-level audit logs but no transaction logs (the transaction logs were overwritten). Management needs an honest data loss assessment.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Provide management with an honest assessment: 3 of 5 days can be fully recovered via transaction logs; days 4-5 will require partial reconstruction from application audit logs with some data loss; begin restoration from the 5-day-old clean backup.

options.label: B
options.text: Start the restoration without informing management of the data gap to avoid difficult conversations.

node-3.title: Staged Recovery Execution
node-3.situation: The 5-day-old backup has been restored. Transaction log replay for days 1-3 is in progress and proceeding correctly. For days 4-5, the team is working to extract records from application audit logs and recreate them.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Monitor the transaction log replay for correctness, coordinate with application teams to verify reconstructed data from audit logs, and keep stakeholders updated on recovery progress and expected data completeness.

options.label: B
options.text: Rush the recovery and skip validation of the transaction log replay to complete restoration faster.

node-4.title: Post-Recovery Actions
node-4.situation: Recovery is complete with 3 days of full data and partial reconstruction of days 4-5. The immediate incident is resolved. The backup system must be fixed to prevent future restore failures.
node-4.question: What is your next step?

options.label: A
options.text: Implement automated backup integrity verification on every backup job, increase transaction log retention to cover at least the full backup retention period, and conduct a full audit of all existing backups to identify any other corrupt archives.

options.label: B
options.text: Only fix the backup integrity checking and leave transaction log retention unchanged.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: You managed the restore failure by systematically testing backups for integrity, transparently communicating the data gap to management, executing a staged recovery using transaction logs and audit log reconstruction, and implementing comprehensive backup verification controls.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
